ccel.title: NPNF-212. Leo the Great, Gregory the Great

source.title: NPNF-212. Leo the Great, Gregory the Great

title: To Venantius, Patrician.

source.title: NPNF-212. Leo the Great, Gregory the Great

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Gregory to Venantius, Patrician, and Ex-monk.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Your communication to us has found us much distressed from having become aware that offence has arisen between you and John our brother and fellow-bishop, in whose agreement with you we were desirous of rejoicing.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For, whatever the cause may have been, rage ought not to have broken out to such a pitch that your armed men, as we have heard, should have burst into the episcopal palace, and committed divers evil deeds in a hostile manner, and that this affair should meanwhile separate you from his paternal charity.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Could not the dispute, whatever it may have been, have been quietly arranged, so that neither party might suffer disadvantage, nor good feeling be disturbed?

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Now it is not unknown to us of what gravity, of what holiness, of what gentleness our above-named brother is.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Whence we gather that, unless excessive force of vexation had compelled him, his Fraternity would by no means have resorted to the measure by which you say that you are aggrieved.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: We, however, on hearing of it by letter from him, at once wrote to him, admonishing him to receive your offerings as before, and not only to allow masses to be celebrated in your house, but, if you wish it, even to officiate himself, and that he ought to have prosecuted his cause without breach of charity.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: And, inasmuch as we wish none to come or continue to be at variance, we have taken care to renew this same admonition.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Hence it is necessary, dearest son, that you, as becomes sons, should shew him the reverence due to a priest, and not provoke his spirit to anger.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For with whom will you have assured goodwill, if (which God forbid) you are at variance with your priest?

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Wherefore, putting away swelling of spirit, try ye so to transact the causes that ye have one with another that both charity may remain inviolate, and what is to your mutual advantage may be peaceably attained.
